data.title: American Express (NYSE: AXP) to report Q3 earnings on Oct. 17, expected at $4.00/share, revenue of $18.05B (up from $16.64B). Stock fell 2.3% to $323.12 on Thursday.
data.description: The American Express Company (NYSE: AXP) is set to announce its third-quarter earnings on October 17, before the market opens. Analysts predict earnings to be $4.00 per share, an increase from $3.49 per share a year ago. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be $18.05 billion, up from $16.64 billion in the previous year. American Express has surpassed revenue expectations for the last three consecutive quarters and in six of the last 10 quarters. On Thursday, the company's shares closed at $323.12, down by 2.3%. For further details and analyst ratings, visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Tomorrowland 2025 unites "People of Tomorrow" in Boom, Belgium, attracting hundreds of thousands to celebrate music and connection.
data.description: In Shenzhen, China, on October 17, 2025, it was reported that Tomorrowland, known for being more than just a music festival, serves as a global congregation where hundreds of thousands of "People of Tomorrow" convene in Boom, Belgium every summer to commemorate electronic music, creativity, and community. The festival draws in a significant number of attendees from around the world annually.

data.title: Dow falls over 300 points, Investor fear rises, Fear & Greed Index hits 'Extreme Fear' zone. Nov data shows -12.8 Philly Fed Manufacturing Index.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index experienced a rise in fear, entering the "Extreme Fear" zone on Thursday. U.S. stocks ended lower, with the Dow Jones index dropping over 300 points. Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE: SCHW) posted strong third-quarter earnings. The Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index dropped to -12.8 in October, hitting a six-month low compared to the previous month's 23.2 and falling short of the estimated 10. For a detailed report, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Circus SE to enter defense sector with AI robotics; new factory in Europe for production of CA-M troop supply system. Go-live in 2026.
data.description: Circus SE (XETRA: CA1), a renowned global technology company specializing in AI robotics for autonomous nutrition systems and troop supply, is enhancing its global production network for entering the defense sector at a high-volume level. With the aim of scaling up production of the CA-M, the world’s pioneering fully autonomous AI robotic system for troop supply, Circus SE is establishing a new European factory. Slated to be operational in 2026, this second factory will significantly boost defense product manufacturing.

data.title: WNS closes acquisition by Capgemini, following terms of agreement dated July 6, 2025.
data.description: In a significant business move, WNS has confirmed the completion of its acquisition by Capgemini S.E. The transaction, formalized on July 6, 2025, solidifies the partnership between the two companies. This acquisition brings together major players in the industry, with WNS being a prominent entity in New York, London, and Mumbai, India. The successful closing of this deal marks a new chapter in the companies' growth strategies and market presence.

data.title: Municipality Finance's redeemed notes delisted from Nasdaq Helsinki trading.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.8%, the lowest it has been in five years. This improvement is credited to the creation of 250,000 new jobs in various sectors. The decrease in the number of unemployed individuals has led to a more stable economy and a positive outlook for the workforce. The government aims to continue this trend by implementing policies that encourage job growth and investment in key industries. Additionally, the report highlighted a surge in consumer spending, which has bolstered economic recovery efforts. The declining unemployment rate signals progress in the country's labor market and provides hope for future job seekers.
